things.name: BayArena
things.description: Home of Bayer 04 Leverkusen, the Bundesliga champions of 2024 who went an entire domestic season unbeaten

things.name: Schloss Morsbroich
things.description: Eighteenth-century baroque castle set in a landscaped park, now housing Leverkusen's contemporary art museum

things.name: Bayer Cultural Park
things.description: Expansive green park created by the pharmaceutical giant Bayer with sports facilities, gardens and open-air events

things.name: Cologne Cathedral
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Gothic masterpiece and Germany's most visited monument, just 20 minutes from Leverkusen by train

things.name: Rheinpark Leverkusen
things.description: Riverside park along the Rhine with walking paths, cycling routes and views across to Cologne's skyline

things.name: Chempark Leverkusen
things.description: One of Europe's largest chemical parks, the industrial heartland where Bayer was founded and aspirin invented in 1897

things.name: Old Market Leverkusen
things.description: Central marketplace of the Opladen district with weekly markets, cafes and the historic heart of old Leverkusen

things.name: Museum Kunstpalast Düsseldorf
things.description: Major art museum with 100,000 works spanning antiquity to the present in Düsseldorf's elegant Ehrenhof park, easily reachable by train

things.name: Gut Neuenhof
things.description: Historic country estate and equestrian centre in the Leverkusen countryside offering guided tours of the baroque manor house

things.name: Altenberg Cathedral
things.description: Extraordinary Cistercian Gothic cathedral in the Bergisches Land hills, with Europe's largest stained-glass window
